skill.name: 3d-web-experience
skill.description: Expert in building 3D experiences for the web - Three.js, React Three Fiber, Spline, WebGL, and interactive 3D scenes. Covers product configurators, 3D portfolios, immersive websites, and bringing designs to life with modern 3D web technologies.
skill.summary: Build immersive 3D web experiences with Three.js, React Three Fiber, and Spline
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation file containing markdown with code examples for 3D web development. All static findings are false positives: (1) The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code block delimiters misidentified as Ruby backticks; (2) The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are false matches on context words; (3) The single URL is a legitimate Spline CDN example. No actual security risks present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown code fence delimiters (```) as Ruby/shell backtick command execution. This is a documentation file with code examples, not executable code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown code fences (```) used to format example code in documentation. No actual shell or Ruby commands are executed by this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly detected 'weak cryptographic algorithms' in context text. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains no cryptographic code or algorithms. This appears to be a false positive pattern match on words in the documentation text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Legitimate CDN URL in Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: A URL to Spline CDN appears in example code showing how to use the Spline library. This is standard usage for this 3D tool, not data exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL 'https://prod.spline.design/xxx/scene.splinecode' is a legitimate example from Spline documentation. It is example code showing correct library usage, not malicious network activity.

content.value_statement: Build stunning 3D websites and web apps using Three.js, React Three Fiber, and Spline. This skill helps you create interactive product configurators, immersive portfolios, and engaging 3D interfaces that work across all devices.
actual_capabilities: Implement Three.js 3D scenes and interactive experiences
Build React applications with React Three Fiber
Optimize 3D models and scenes for web performance
Integrate Spline designs into web projects
Add scroll-driven animations and interactions to 3D scenes
limitations: Does not create 3D models or assets - only implements them in code
Requires existing 3D assets (GLB/GLTF files) for full implementation
Performance depends on device capabilities - cannot override hardware limits

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Viewer
use_cases.description: Create interactive 3D product configurators that let customers rotate, zoom, and customize products before purchase.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce developers building product visualization features

use_cases.title: Creative Portfolio Enhancement
use_cases.description: Add impressive 3D elements to personal or agency portfolios to stand out from static websites.
use_cases.target_user: Web designers and creative professionals showcasing their work

use_cases.title: Immersive Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Build engaging landing pages with scroll-driven 3D animations that create memorable user experiences.

prompt_templates.title: Basic 3D Scene Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic 3D scene using Three.js with a rotating cube, ambient lighting, and orbit controls. Explain the key components.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new 3D web project from scratch

prompt_templates.title: React Three Fiber Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to integrate a GLB 3D model into a React app using React Three Fiber. Include loading state and error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a 3D model to an existing React application

prompt_templates.title: 3D Product Configurator
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a product configurator that lets users change colors and textures on a 3D model. Include UI controls outside the canvas.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating an e-commerce product customization feature

prompt_templates.title: Scroll-Driven 3D Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3D scene where camera movement and model rotation are controlled by scroll position. Use R3F ScrollControls.

output_examples.input: Create a React Three Fiber scene with a 3D model loader
output_examples.output:
A complete code example showing:
- Canvas setup with proper lighting
- Suspense wrapper for async loading
- useGLTF hook for model loading
- Progress indicator during load
- OrbitControls for user interaction
- Error boundary for failed loads

output_examples.input: How do I optimize 3D models for web?
output_examples.output:
Step-by-step guide covering:
1. Reduce polygon count in Blender (under 100K)
2. Bake textures into single materials
3. Export as GLB/GLTF format
4. Compress with gltf-transform using draco
5. Target under 5MB file size
6. Use CDN for delivery

best_practices: Always include loading states and progress indicators for 3D content
Test 3D experiences on real mobile devices, not just desktop browsers
Provide static image fallbacks for users on low-end devices or with reduced motion preferences
anti_patterns: Adding 3D elements purely for visual effect without functional purpose
Implementing 3D that only works on desktop browsers
Failing to provide any loading feedback while 3D content loads

faq.question: What is the best way to start learning 3D web development?
faq.answer: Start with Spline for quick results if you are new to 3D. Move to React Three Fiber when you need more control. Learn vanilla Three.js last for maximum flexibility.

faq.question: How do I make my 3D site work on mobile?
faq.answer: Test on real devices. Reduce model complexity. Lower pixel ratio on mobile. Consider disabling 3D on very low-end devices. Always provide fallback content.

faq.question: What 3D model formats work best for web?
faq.answer: GLB and GLTF are the standard formats. They are small, well-supported, and include everything in one file. Avoid FBX or OBJ for web.

faq.question: How do I handle 3D loading times?
faq.answer: Show progress with useProgress hook. Display skeleton or placeholder. Lazy load 3D after main content. Compress models aggressively.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with any JavaScript framework?
faq.answer: React Three Fiber works with React. For Vue, use TroisJS or Vue-Threejs. For vanilla JS, use Three.js directly. Spline works in any framework.

faq.question: How do I add scroll-driven animations to my 3D scene?
faq.answer: Use @react-three/drei ScrollControls. Access scroll.offset in useFrame to drive animations. Combine with GSAP ScrollTrigger for advanced effects.
